subject: english
learning_objectives: Identify the consonant sounds taught in the lesson
Recognise spelling patterns for the sounds
Contrast pairs of words containing the target sounds

sections.heading: Dental Fricatives
sections.content: The note contrasts the voiceless and voiced 'th' sounds with examples such as think, thank, thin, thumb, author, path, than, they, father, and those.

sections.heading: Alveolar Plosives
sections.content: It also contrasts /t/ and /d/ through spelling variants and examples such as two, ten, Thomas, thyme, attack, matter, do, den, daddy, ladder, and middle.

sections.heading: Contrast Practice
sections.content: Sample contrasting pairs in the note include thank/than, think/this, thin/the, anthem/father, slapped/wanted, kicked/expected, ten/den, two/do, latter/ladder, and battle/paddle.
